assertions.expectation: Checks for deprecated API usage that may have changed behavior in 1.30
assertions.reason: Response includes specific commands to check for deprecated API calls: 'kubectl get events --all-namespaces | grep -i deprecat' and checking application logs for API warnings

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking admission webhooks — they may be rejecting or slowing down requests on the new version
assertions.reason: Response mentions checking 'Admission controller configuration changes' in the troubleshooting list

assertions.expectation: Advises checking if resource requests/limits need adjusting for any system components that changed
assertions.reason: Response includes commands to check control plane resource limits and node resource utilization: 'kubectl describe pod -n kube-system kube-apiserver-<node>' and resource allocation checks

assertions.expectation: Suggests examining kube-system pods for any that are crashlooping or restarting
assertions.reason: Response includes commands to check kube-system pods status: 'kubectl get pods -n kube-system | grep apiserver' and 'kubectl get pods -n kube-system | grep etcd'

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to check API server metrics and latency (kubectl get --raw /metrics)
assertions.reason: Response includes specific commands: 'kubectl get --raw /metrics | grep apiserver_request_duration' and 'kubectl get --raw /metrics | grep apiserver_request_total'

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking if NetworkPolicy or service mesh components need updating for the new version
assertions.reason: Response mentions 'Network policy changes affecting internal cluster communication' as something to look for

assertions.expectation: Advises reviewing pod events for scheduling delays or readiness probe failures
assertions.reason: Response includes 'kubectl get events --all-namespaces' command and mentions checking service endpoints, though it doesn't specifically mention readiness probe failures

assertions.expectation: Mentions checking if HPA or VPA are behaving differently on the new version
assertions.reason: Response does not mention HPA (Horizontal Pod Autoscaler) or VPA (Vertical Pod Autoscaler) considerations for the version upgrade
